data.narrative:
Neros is a Series B-stage defense and security company founded by Soren Monroe-Anderson and Olaf Hichwa that has raised $121M to date. The valuation analysis draws on a comparable universe of 10 peer companies—2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private entities in the same sector and stage, including Vannevar Labs, Dream Security, Harmattan AI, Castelion, and Defense Unicorns.

Two triangulation methods were applied. The stage-progression method uses $121M in total raised as a proxy base value and applies a 1.9x multiplier typical of Series B advancement, yielding $230M. The peer-median approach examines six same-sector, same-stage private comparables with valuations ranging from $1.0B to $1.5B, producing a median of $1.2B. This wide dispersion reflects heterogeneity within the defense tech space at this stage.

Synthesizing both methods across the full peer set produces a triangulated range of $195M (low) to $1.4B (high), with a median of $715M. The stage-progression result sits at the lower bound, while peer multiples anchor the upper portion. The material gap between these methods reflects uncertainty around Neros's relative progress and go-to-market traction versus named comparables. Resolution of valuation within this range depends on revenue scale, customer concentration, and technical differentiation relative to the peer cohort.
